reasoning_model_1: The model attempts to incorporate the required paralinguistic feature by including a non-speech sound '[Laugh]' at the beginning (00:00), which supports the 'contemptuous' (轻蔑) attitude instruction. However, the model fails significantly in Content Accuracy. At timestamp 00:06, it inserts the phrase '请注意' (Please note), which is not part of the requested script ('请原谅我的失态'). This appears to be a hallucination or a leakage of system instructions into the speech. This insertion breaks the natural flow of the apology and alters the sentence structure, constituting a major issue. While the emotional transition is attempted, the content error renders the generation flawed.
reasoning_model_2: The model demonstrates perfect Content Accuracy, reciting the script exactly as requested without any hallucinations, omissions, or mispronunciations. The timing suggests a distinct separation between the contemptuous segment (00:00-00:03) and the polite apology starting at 00:04. Although it lacks the explicit non-speech marker '[Laugh]' found in Model 1, it successfully delivers the content fluently. By avoiding the critical content errors seen in the other model, it achieves a standard of 'Partially correct' or better, effectively conveying the text with the requested structure.
model_comparison: Model 1 attempts a more expressive performance (adding a laugh) but fails critically on content accuracy by hallucinating the phrase '请注意' (Please note) into the middle of the script, likely reading part of an instruction. This makes the audio unnatural and incorrect relative to the prompt. Model 2 strictly adheres to the provided text with no errors. In evaluation, content accuracy is a primary constraint; a model that speaks the wrong text (Model 1) is inferior to one that speaks the correct text (Model 2), even if Model 1 attempted more complex paralinguistic features. Therefore, Model 2 wins.
